claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me first review the official eligibility rules, and then I'll ask you about your specific situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. Let me start by asking you a few questions.

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex, who is 5, Blake, who is 17, and Charlie, who is 19. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! You have three children - Alex (5), Blake (17), and Charlie (19). Let me ask you some questions to assess eligibility for each of them.

Let me start with some general questions:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now let me ask about each child individually:

**Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about Blake (17) and Charlie (19) specifically, as there are additional requirements for children aged 16 and over.

**Is Blake (who is 17) currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask about Blake's situation:

**Did Blake leave education or training recently, and if so, has Blake registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake hasn't registered with the careers service or armed services.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now let me ask about Charlie:

**Is Charlie (who is 19) currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie is in approved education - studying for A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now let me ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year?**

(Note: This won't affect your eligibility, but it's important to know as it may affect whether you need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not go into details about my income right now - I'm mainly just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim in the first place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's absolutely fine - I have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. Let me review the situation for each of your children based on the rules.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

Based on the information you've provided, here's the outcome for each of your children:

**✅ Alex (age 5) - ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. As a child under 16 who lives with you in the UK, Alex meets all the basic eligibility requirements.

**❌ Blake (age 17) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, you are not currently eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. For children aged 16 or over, they must be under 20 AND in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in education or training and hasn't registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake at this time.

**✅ Charlie (age 19) - ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Even though Charlie is 19, you can still claim because Charlie is under 20 and currently in approved education (studying for A-levels).

**Overall:** You can claim Child Benefit for 2 out of your 3 children - Alex and Charlie.

**Important note about Blake:** If Blake decides to return to approved education or training, or registers with a careers service or the armed services, you may become eligible to claim for Blake again. You would need to contact the Child Benefit Office if Blake's circumstances change.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit claim?

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16. You live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the basic eligibility requirements for a child under 16. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 17 years old. For children aged 16 or over, you can only claim Child Benefit if they are under 20 AND in approved education or training. Blake is not currently in approved education or training. You could have claimed for up to 20 weeks after Blake left education if Blake had registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, but Blake has not done so. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 19 years old and is currently in approved education (studying for A-levels). For children aged 16 or over, you can claim Child Benefit if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training. Charlie meets these requirements - under 20 and in approved education. You live in the UK, Charlie lives with you, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children. Alex (5) meets all basic requirements for Child Benefit as a child under 16. Blake (17) does not qualify because, despite being under 20, Blake is not in approved education or training and has not registered with careers services or armed services. Charlie (19) qualifies because Charlie is under 20 and currently in approved education. The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for 2 out of 3 children (Alex and Charlie).

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 5 years old, which is under 16. You live in the UK, Alex lives with you, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the basic eligibility requirements for a child under 16. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 17 years old. For children aged 16 or over, you can only claim Child Benefit if they are under 20 AND in approved education or training. Blake is not currently in approved education or training. You could have claimed for up to 20 weeks after Blake left education if Blake had registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services, but Blake has not done so. Therefore, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 19 years old and is currently in approved education (studying for A-levels). For children aged 16 or over, you can claim Child Benefit if they are under 20 and stay in approved education or training. Charlie meets these requirements - under 20 and in approved education. You live in the UK, Charlie lives with you, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Therefore, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children. Alex (5) meets all basic requirements for Child Benefit as a child under 16. Blake (17) does not qualify because, despite being under 20, Blake is not in approved education or training and has not registered with careers services or armed services. Charlie (19) qualifies because Charlie is under 20 and currently in approved education. The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for 2 out of 3 children (Alex and Charlie).
